title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
problem_statement: Design a data structure that supports insert, delete, and getRandom in average O(1) time.

examples.input: insert(1), insert(2), getRandom(), remove(1), insert(2), getRandom()
examples.explanation: Insert and remove use a hash map and list, while getRandom returns any element with equal probability.

constraints: getRandom is only called when the set is non-empty
intuition: Use a list for random access and a hash map from value to index to support constant-time remove by swapping the deleted element with the list tail.

approaches.name: ArrayList plus HashMap
approaches.description: Keep values in a list and store their indices in a map. Remove by swapping the target with the last element, update the map, and pop from the list.
approaches.why_not: A balanced tree can support delete and insert but cannot provide uniform random access in O(1).

interview_qa.a: Swap the element to remove with the last element in the list, update the index map, and pop the list tail.
Pick a random index from 0 to list.size()-1 so every stored element has equal probability.
